mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds a high threshold, it signals peak cyclical pessimism and that the worst of an economic downturn has passed. Subsequent policy support and labor market mean-reversion drive a recovery, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 9.8% in Sep 2009, a multi-decade high
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 50% of instances where UNRATE is above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate provides a persistent tailwind for equities by lowering the discount rate for future earnings and encouraging portfolio reallocation towards riskier assets. This effect is continuous as long as the zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) is maintained.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has held rates in 0-0.25% range since Dec 2008
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return during the ZIRP period is not statistically different from the average return when FEDFUNDS > 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of severely depressed housing starts and negative retail sales growth indicates a deep consumer recession has already occurred and is priced in. Any stabilization from this low base acts as a powerful positive catalyst for forward returns as worst-case fears subside.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production indicates a recovery in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy. This translates directly into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO bottomed in June 2009 and is recovering
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the rate of job losses, measured by the month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls, is a leading indicator of a labor market bottom. This signals the end of a recession and prompts a forward-looking equity rally.
evidence_refs: fact:Monthly job losses peaked at over 800k in early 2009 and have since moderated
failure_condition: When the rate of job loss slows past the trigger, the S&P 500 has negative returns over the next 60 days in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in inflation while unemployment remains high creates a stagflationary risk. This dynamic constrains the Federal Reserve's ability to remain accommodative, increasing uncertainty and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: In periods matching the trigger condition, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive short-term changes in retail sales provide a high-frequency signal of consumer spending strength. This unexpected strength leads to immediate upward revisions in revenue forecasts and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates are at the zero lower bound, falling inflation (disinflation) is bearish for equities. It signals weak aggregate demand and increases the real burden of debt, depressing economic activity and corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Deflation was a major concern during the GFC
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.
